verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-related peptidase 2) is a secreted serine protease expressed primarily in prostate epithelium. It is synthesized as a preproprotein, processed, and secreted into the extracellular milieu (seminal plasma, bloodstream). It has a classical signal peptide but no transmembrane domain and no GPI anchor — it is a purely secreted protein. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/stress/apoptosis outer-leaflet ecto-pool for KLK2 protein body; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — KLK2 has no TM or GPI anchor in any tissue, so no surface display even in prostate; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM minority pool or vesicular cycling to surface; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no evidence of wash-resistant covalent or non-covalent tethering to a TM scaffold on cell surfaces. Clinical antibody programs target circulating/soluble KLK2, not cell-surface-displayed protein body. Verdict: secreted_only / no.
